commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

feat(data-browsing): Use custom React chart legend component in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization` (#108493) Replaces the default ECharts SVG legend with an interactive React component in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization`. The ECharts legend has usability issues — users don't realize items are clickable, and the scrollable ribbon doesn't handle overflow well. This custom version is more powerful. It sits in `TimeSeriesWidgetVisualization` because it needs to rely on and be aware of various time series coordination problems (colour palettes, etc.) that don't apply in other contexts. Maybe in the future I can expand this to be used in other places, but the main motivator by far is to use this in Dashboards and Explore. **e.g.,** The new `ChartLegend` renders a row of colored checkboxes that toggle series visibility. Items that don't fit are kept in the DOM (hidden with `visibility: hidden` so they still contribute to width measurement) and shown in an overflow dropdown instead. A `ResizeObserver` on both the container and the overflow trigger re-measures whenever available space changes. State updates from the observer are deferred via `scheduleMicroTask` to avoid forced reflows. [Improved Chart Legend](https://linear.app/getsentry/project/improved-chart-legend-e0d2083d7197/overview) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix upload - use base64 JSON instead of FormData

Clerk middleware v7 corrupts multipart/form-data routing in Next.js 16
Turbopack, making FormData uploads impossible. Switched to base64 JSON
approach which completely bypasses the issue:

- Frontend: FileReader converts PDF to base64, sends as JSON POST
- Backend: receives JSON body, decodes base64 to Buffer, parses PDF
- Middleware: reverted to simple Clerk middleware (no bypass needed)
- Removed test-upload route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: treat SMSMasivos verification_03 as success (SMS already sent)

Root cause: when SMS was already sent to a phone number, API returns
success:false with code:verification_03. Our code treated this as failure
and generated a fallback code, hiding the real SMS that was delivered.

Now verification_03 is treated as success — no fallback code shown,
user must enter the code from the SMS they already received.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ACME server (RFC 8555) and README

Implements a full ACME protocol server so clients like certbot, acme.sh,
Caddy, and Traefik can automatically obtain and renew certificates from the
internal CA without manual intervention.

- ACME protocol endpoints at /acme/ (directory, nonce, account, order,
  authz, challenge, finalize, certificate download)
- http-01 challenge verification via httpx background task
- Skip-challenge option for internal hostnames unreachable over HTTP
- RSA and ECDSA JWS/JWK support (RFC 7515/7638) in acme_jws.py
- X-Forwarded-Host/Proto support for reverse proxy deployments
- Management API at /api/acme/ (list/delete accounts)
- ACME page in UI: enable toggle, CA selector, cert validity, directory URL
  with copy button and client examples, registered account table
- Settings passthrough so SMTP saves don't clobber ACME config
- New DB tables: acme_accounts, acme_orders, acme_authorizations,
  acme_challenges, acme_nonces (auto-migrated on startup)
- Add httpx dependency

Add --warnings-as-errors flag to BUnit test runner (#1202)

## Summary
- Add `warnings_as_errors` field to `CompilerOptions` that promotes
warnings/hints to errors
- Wire `--warnings-as-errors` CLI flag through the `beamtalk test`
command
- Enable `--warnings-as-errors` by default in the Justfile `test-bunit`
recipe
- Fix all existing test warnings with `@expect` directives:
- `http_client_test.bt`: `@expect all` for intentional Actor `new` usage
- `http_test.bt`: `@expect dnu` for `bodyAsJson -> Object` (at: valid on
Dictionary/List at runtime)
- `subprocess_test.bt`: `@expect dnu` for `System osFamily = "unix"`
comparison

- [x] `cargo build` compiles cleanly
- [x] `just clippy` passes
- [x] All 122 BUnit test files compile with `--warnings-as-errors` (no
stale `@expect` directives)

commit.message:
fix(x-agent): resolve Gemini partial inference fallback (5.0/5.0 scores) (#75)

Root causes:
1. max_tokens=4096 insufficient — with 7 dimensions + competitive analysis,
   Gemini truncates the JSON response mid-object → parse failure → 5.0 defaults
2. "Think step by step" outside JSON block — caused Gemini to write narrative
   text before JSON, consuming tokens before the JSON output (Z/CS don't have this)

Fix:
- Bump X Agent max_tokens 4096 → 8192 (consistent with Z and CS Sentinel bump)
- Remove freeform "Think step by step" instruction; reasoning is captured inside
  reasoning_steps array (one step per dimension, as it should be)
- Rewrite JSON format hint to show explicit per-dimension step examples
- Add "Do NOT output any text before or after the JSON" guardrail

Expected: X Agent returns real innovation/strategic scores instead of 5.0 fallback.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(admin): refresh expired session before loading users

getSession() only reads cached tokens from localStorage — if the access
token expired (tab was inactive, returning after hours), queries silently
hang. Now refreshSession() validates with the server first in both
AuthContext (on initial load) and AdminUsers (before data queries).
Retries also cover JWT-related errors with backoff.

commit.message:
fix: production hardening — safety, observability, dead code, config validation (#484)

* fix: prevent coordinator reconnects from GetExternalIP spam and spurious netlink events

Two root causes of coordinator mesh reconnects in Docker-compose environments:

1. GetExternalIP() was called on every 10s heartbeat. In Docker containers
   (private IPs only), this triggered HTTP requests to ifconfig.me/ipify on
   every heartbeat. Intermittent failures caused the returned IP to oscillate,
   triggering IPsChanged() → DisconnectAll().

   Fix: add a permanent in-process cache (extIPCache/extIPValid). The result
   (even "") is cached until ResetExternalIPCache() is called explicitly from
   HandleNetworkChange, ensuring a single fetch per genuine network event.

2. shouldIgnore() on Linux and Darwin passed events with Interface="" through
   to HandleNetworkChange. On Linux, IPv6 RTM_NEWADDR events lack IFA_LABEL
   so ifName stays ""; on Darwin, InterfaceByIndex() failures produce the same
   result. These unidentifiable events triggered spurious DisconnectAll().

   Fix: change shouldIgnore to return true for empty Interface on Linux and
   Darwin. Add an IfAddrmsg.Index fallback in the Linux netlink parser so real
   IPv6 address changes on identifiable interfaces still pass through correctly.
   Windows is intentionally unchanged (its callback structurally has no iface).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prune replicated state vectors and drop empty bucket index entries

Two remaining memory-leak fixes after #475:

1. repl_queue.go: PruneDeletedKeys now uses allKeys (local + replicated)
   instead of localKeys when building the activeSet. Previously, state
   vectors for replicated objects were never eligible for pruning because
   localKeys excluded peer-owned buckets. After deletion on the primary,
   a replica could accumulate N accordion-run worth of stale vectors
   (~9-12 MB) before they aged out. Now deleted replicated objects are
   pruned after GCGracePeriod (10 min) just like local ones.

2. admin_s3_listing.go: updateListingIndex("remove") now deletes the
   bucket key from the map when both Objects and Recycled are empty after
   the removal, rather than leaving a zero-byte entry that reconcileLocalIndex
   would clean up 5 min later. Negligible individual impact (~36 bytes/bucket)
   but keeps the index map tidy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: relay JWT refresh on 401 + S3Down alert false positive

Two bugs caused a relay peer to stay permanently stuck disconnected after
a JWT token expiry, and a Prometheus alert to fire falsely during idle periods.

Relay JWT expiry fix (Bug A + Bug B):
- Add `ErrUnauthorized` sentinel to `coord.ErrUnauthorized` so 401 responses
  can be identified by callers
- Detect HTTP 401 in `PersistentRelay.Connect()` and wrap as `ErrUnauthorized`
  (previously returned a generic error, so the error handler ignored it)
- Add `PersistentRelay.UpdateJWTToken()` to atomically replace the stored JWT;
  fixes the data race where `Connect()` read `jwtToken` without holding the lock
- Expand `onReconnectError` handler in `node.go` to handle both 404 and 401:
  re-registers with the coordinator and pushes the fresh JWT into the relay so
  the next `autoReconnect` attempt uses the new token instead of the expired one

S3Down alert fix:
- Remove idle-traffic leg (`sum(rate(...)) == 0`) from the alert expression;
  the alert now fires only when metrics are absent, not when S3 is idle
- Update summary/description to reflect the narrowed scope

Tests:
- `TestPersistentRelay_Connect_401_ReturnsErrUnauthorized`: verifies 401 → ErrUnauthorized
- `TestPersistentRelay_UpdateJWTToken`: verifies token replacement takes effect on next Connect
- `TestSetupRelayHandlers_ErrUnauthorized_TriggersReregistration`: end-to-end test of
  the full 401 → re-register → reconnect-with-fresh-token flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: close resp.Body on WebSocket dial error

Per gorilla/websocket docs, the response body must be closed by the
caller when DialContext returns an error with a non-nil response.
Previously the body was read but never closed, leaking connections
during rapid reconnect loops.

Caught in code review of #484.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: fix flaky TestPersistentRelay_UpdateJWTToken

The original test created two relays and asserted that the last token
received by the server was "new-token". This was racy: relay1's
autoReconnect goroutine could fire after relay2's initial connect,
making the last-seen token "old-token" instead.

Rewrite to use a single relay: call UpdateJWTToken before Connect,
then verify the server sees the new token on the first (and only)
connection attempt. No ordering dependency, no background goroutines
racing against the assertion. Verified stable with -race -count=10.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: production hardening - safety, observability, dead code, config validation

Tier 1 - Safety/Correctness:
- relay.go: Add closed-connection check in QueryFilterRules to prevent
  queuing messages on dead persistent connections
- relay.go: Remove ctx parameter from newRelayManager (was silently discarded)
- relay.go: Remove unnecessary goroutine spawning for BroadcastPeerReconnected,
  PushFilterRules, PushServicePorts — all are non-blocking channel sends
- node.go: Add jitter to relay reconnect backoff to prevent thundering herd
  when N peers simultaneously lose the relay
- main.go: Add 10s timeout to srv.Shutdown() to prevent indefinite block
- main.go: Add safeGo() helper with panic recovery for long-running goroutines;
  use it for coordinator server goroutine
- transport/udp: Add per-packet panic recovery in packetWorker so a single
  malformed packet cannot crash the worker goroutine
- main.go: Reorder shutdown — resolver.Shutdown() before CloseAll() to avoid
  orphaned in-flight DNS queries

Tier 2 - Observability:
- metrics: Add DroppedQueueFull counter (tunnelmesh_dropped_queue_full_total)
- transport/udp: Increment counter when packet queue is full; expose
  SetOnDropQueueFull() setter wired from main.go after metrics init
- alerts.yml: Add ElevatedUDPQueueDrops alert (warning, >10/s for 2m)
- alerts.yml: Fix S3Down — raise threshold 2m→5m, add peer-count guard
  to avoid false positives during normal maintenance restarts
- node.go: Add OpenTelemetry span to ReconnectPersistentRelay (records
  attempt count, relay URL, error outcome)

Tier 3 - Dead/Redundant Code:
- persistent_relay.go: Track consecutive write failures in writeLoop;
  after 3 failures close the connection to force reconnect

Config Validation:
- config.go: Validate MetricsPort is in 1–65535 range (0 = use default)
- config.go: Validate HeartbeatInterval is a parseable positive duration

Tests:
- config_test.go: Add table-driven cases for MetricsPort and HeartbeatInterval validation
- relay_test.go: Add TestRelayManager_QueryFilterRules_ConnectionClosed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback on hardening changes

- Apply backoff jitter before the maxBackoff cap in ReconnectPersistentRelay
  (two sites) so total wait stays within bounds rather than exceeding by 25%
- Add TOCTOU comment to QueryFilterRules closed-check clarifying it is a
  best-effort pre-flight, not a strict synchronization guarantee
- Add comment in writeLoop explaining that closing conn triggers readLoop
  reconnect via shouldReconnect=true path
- Add TestPersistentRelay_WriteLoop_ClosesAfterConsecutiveFailures covering
  the consecutive-failure detection behaviour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address second round of PR review feedback

- Simplify MetricsPort validation: remove the redundant != 0 guard and
  use < 0 instead of < 1 so the condition correctly rejects negative values
  without accidentally implying port 0 is valid by a different path
- Add advanceBackoff closure in ReconnectPersistentRelay to eliminate the
  identical jitter+cap formula duplicated across two retry sites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: fix race in TestPersistentRelay_WriteLoop_ClosesAfterConsecutiveFailures

The previous version triggered a data race between:
- SendTo() reading p.writeChan under RLock then sending to it without the lock
- autoReconnect's new readLoop closing that same channel under a write Lock

Root cause: when the test server's handler had closeSig already closed, it
accepted the reconnect WebSocket upgrade before immediately dropping the TCP
connection. This caused Connect() to succeed and create a new writeChan, then
the new readLoop immediately exited and closed that writeChan — racing with the
test goroutine's SendTo calls.

Fix: reject all reconnection attempts with 503 so that Connect() returns an
error before creating a new writeChan. With no new writeChan ever created,
p.writeChan stays nil after the original readLoop's defer sets it to nil, and
SendTo returns ErrNotConnected without any channel close/send race.

Also remove the SendTo loop that was racing; the disconnect is reliably
detected via readLoop's shouldReconnect path when the TCP conn is dropped
without a WebSocket close frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

repository.name: tunnelmesh
repository.description: Creates an encrypted mesh of tunnels between peers using SSH, UDP or a custom Relay protocol
